text:
There once was a little girl named Lily. She lived in a quiet town bathed in sunlight. Every day she'd trot to the nearby pond to play with her best friend, Fluffy.

There was something peculiar about the pond. Whenever Lily dipped her toes into the water, bubbles would emerge from beneath. One day, while giggling, she dove in and disappeared beneath the surface.

Fluffy waited anxiously. Moments later, Lily reappeared, laughing gleefully. "Mommy!" she cried out. But there was no mother there. There was just water, and thousands of tiny, glittering bugs.

She hurried home, alarmed. Her father hugged her.

"A dragon?" she exclaimed.

"Yeah," he chuckled. "But there's no dragon."

That night, Lily dreamt of bright dragons dancing through the bubbles and singing sweet songs.

When she woke up the next morning, she discovered a tiny, glowing key next to her pillow. Her heart raced with anticipation. She picked it
